response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins which are not present in water  
the body's immune system is designed to respond to proteins which are not found in water  
the immune system of people allergic to peanuts is not adapted to the chemical composition of water  
peanuts contain proteins that are not soluble in water and cannot be absorbed by the body in an aquatic environment  

Sec. 2  
the chemical structure of peanuts is different from that of water and does not support the survival of allergic individuals  
the body cannot process the chemical properties of water and cannot accommodate the presence of peanut proteins in an aquatic setting  
the immune system of allergic individuals is not equipped to handle the physical and chemical properties of water  
the inability to live underwater is a result of the mismatch between the body's physiological needs and the environment of water
